parameters.content:
### Receive Inbound Webhook

Entry point: listens for POST calls on `/wf05051-draft`. Use the TEST url while building, switch callers to the PRODUCTION url after publishing.

Next: Stamp & Shape Draft

### Stamp & Shape Draft

Shapes the item: writes `draftId`, `receivedAt`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Payload Valid?

### Payload Valid?

Two-way gate on `={{ $json.draftId }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Classify with AI · ②→ Record Rejected Input

### Record Rejected Input

Appends a new row to 'Draft Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Classify with AI

LLM classification into: Urgent, Routine, FYI (+fallback). Output port order matches this list.

Ports: ①→ Store as Urgent · ②→ Store as Routine, Capture Failure Context · ③→ Store as FYI · ④→ Alert Slack — Other

⚠ failures exit via the error rail

### Groq Chat Model

Chat model powering the AI step (model: `llama-3.3-70b-versatile`). Swap providers here without rewiring.

Feeds **Classify with AI** as `languageModel`

🔑 Groq API key

### Store as Urgent

Appends a new row to 'Draft Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Ports: ①→ Reunite Branches · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Store as Routine

Appends a new row to 'Draft Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Ports: ①→ Reunite Branches · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Store as FYI

Appends a new row to 'Draft Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Ports: ①→ Reunite Branches · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Alert Slack — Other

Posts a message to Slack (`#content`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

Ports: ①→ Reunite Branches · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Reunite Branches

Rejoins 4 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Cap Batch Volume

### Cap Batch Volume

Caps the stream at 50 items to protect downstream quotas.

Next: Summarize Run Stats

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

### Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

Appends a new row to 'Draft Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Outlook · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Send Summary to Outlook

Sends an email via Microsoft Outlook — recipient comes from the item's `email` field.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Outlook

### Escalate Failure to Outlook

Sends an email via Microsoft Outlook — recipient comes from the item's `email` field.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

# Distill documents with Google Drive and Outlook with instant alerts

**Pattern:** AI Summarize & Route · **Level:** Advanced · **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Distill documents with Google Drive and Outlook with instant alerts. It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual social copy is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Receive Inbound Webhook → Stamp & Shape Draft → Payload Valid? → Classify with AI → Store as Urgent → Reunite Branches → Cap Batch Volume → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to ai summarize & route without manual effort
- Anyone automating social copy in content creation
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Has retries and error handling built in
- Removes copy-paste errors
- Scales without extra headcount

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Google OAuth2 / service account; Groq API key; Microsoft OAuth2; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

One Text Classifier node replaces what would otherwise be a chain of brittle IF/Switch keyword checks — the LLM handles the ambiguous cases a rules engine can't. Each category gets its own action node, so a failure in one branch (say, a bad Slack call) never blocks the others; Merge reunites them so the run still reports one clean completion.

## Practice exercises

You've got the basics covered — push further:

1. Add a 4th category with its own action branch.
2. Replace one action (e.g. a Slack ping) with a task-creation node and see how the wiring changes.
3. Feed the classifier deliberately ambiguous text and check which fallback category it lands in.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🔐 Security

Never trust inbound payloads: the webhook path is unguessable. Add header auth or IP allow-listing for defense in depth. Secrets live in n8n credentials — never in node parameters.

## 🤖 AI cluster

Purple nodes form one AI unit: the root node orchestrates, sub-nodes plug in via typed ports (model / memory / tools / parser). Swap the chat model without rewiring anything else. Costs scale with tokens — keep prompts lean and cap iterations.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your social copy process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
